subject: [PATCH] userfaultfd: wait on source PMD during UFFDIO_MOVE

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Thanks for the patch and added a cc:stable tag to it. The patch looks good and should be applied.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sun,  5 Jul 2026 06:12:31 -0700 Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev> wrote:

> move_pages_huge_pmd() snapshots src_pmdval under src_ptl, drops the lock,
> and, for migration entries, waits with pmd_migration_entry_wait().
> 
> Passing &src_pmdval is wrong. pmd_migration_entry_wait() must lock and
> re-read the real page-table PMD; on split-PMD-lock kernels, a stack
> address also resolves to the wrong lock. softleaf_entry_wait_on_locked()
> then waits without a folio reference, which is safe only while serialized
> against migration-entry removal by the real PT lock.
> 
> Pass src_pmd, matching __handle_mm_fault() and hmm_vma_walk_pmd().

Thanks.

> Reported-by: sashiko-bot <sashiko-bot@kernel.org>
> Link: https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260703173903.3789516-1-usama.arif%40linux.dev?part=8

Sashiko says "wild spinlock acquisition and memory corruption", so I'll
add a cc:stable to this.

> --- a/mm/huge_memory.c
> +++ b/mm/huge_memory.c
> @@ -2775,7 +2775,7 @@ int move_pages_huge_pmd(struct mm_struct *mm, pmd_t *dst_pmd, pmd_t *src_pmd, pm
>  	if (!pmd_trans_huge(src_pmdval)) {
>  		spin_unlock(src_ptl);
>  		if (pmd_is_migration_entry(src_pmdval)) {
> -			pmd_migration_entry_wait(mm, &src_pmdval);
> +			pmd_migration_entry_wait(mm, src_pmd);
>  			return -EAGAIN;
>  		}
>  		return -ENOENT;

2026-07-05.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the userfaultfd code, specifically in the move_pages_huge_pmd function. The issue arises from passing the wrong PMD value to pmd_migration_entry_wait, leading to potential memory corruption and incorrect behavior. The fix involves changing the parameter passed to pmd_migration_entry_wait to match the correct PMD.

reviews.author: Rik van Riel
reviews.summary: Reviewed the patch and found it to be correct. The change makes sense and fixes a potential issue with PMD values.

reviews.author: Baolin Wang
reviews.summary: Agreed with Andrew that the patch should be cc'd to stable. Reviewed and found it correct.
reviews.raw_body:
On 7/5/26 9:12 PM, Usama Arif wrote:
> move_pages_huge_pmd() snapshots src_pmdval under src_ptl, drops the lock,
> and, for migration entries, waits with pmd_migration_entry_wait().
> 
> Passing &src_pmdval is wrong. pmd_migration_entry_wait() must lock and
> re-read the real page-table PMD; on split-PMD-lock kernels, a stack
> address also resolves to the wrong lock. softleaf_entry_wait_on_locked()
> then waits without a folio reference, which is safe only while serialized
> against migration-entry removal by the real PT lock.
> 
> Pass src_pmd, matching __handle_mm_fault() and hmm_vma_walk_pmd().
> 
> Reported-by: sashiko-bot <sashiko-bot@kernel.org>

Good catch. Agree with Andrew that we should cc stable.
Reviewed-by: Baolin Wang <baolin.wang@linux.alibaba.com>

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary: Also agreed that the patch should be cc'd to stable. Reviewed and found it correct.
reviews.raw_body:
On 2026/7/5 21:12, Usama Arif wrote:
> move_pages_huge_pmd() snapshots src_pmdval under src_ptl, drops the lock,
> and, for migration entries, waits with pmd_migration_entry_wait().
> 
> Passing &src_pmdval is wrong. pmd_migration_entry_wait() must lock and
> re-read the real page-table PMD; on split-PMD-lock kernels, a stack
> address also resolves to the wrong lock. softleaf_entry_wait_on_locked()

Well spotted!

> then waits without a folio reference, which is safe only while serialized
> against migration-entry removal by the real PT lock.
> 
> Pass src_pmd, matching __handle_mm_fault() and hmm_vma_walk_pmd().
>

With that,

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand (Arm)
reviews.summary: Acked the patch and found it to be correct. The change follows what is done in __handle_mm_fault().

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the userfaultfd code, specifically in the move_pages_huge_pmd function. The issue arises from passing the wrong PMD value to pmd_migration_entry_wait, leading to potential memory corruption and incorrect behavior. The fix involves changing the parameter passed to pmd_migration_entry_wait to match the correct PMD.
